zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret verch Roland於1483年出生在英國威爾斯安格萊波迪德時，她的父親Rowland ap Howell28歲，母親Annes ferch Dafydd20歲。她於1502年與Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn在英國威爾斯安格萊波迪德結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1515年在她的家鄉享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret verch Roland于1483年出生在英国威尔斯安格萊波迪德时，她的父亲Rowland ap Howell28岁，母亲Annes ferch Dafydd20岁。她于1502年与Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn在英国威尔斯安格萊波迪德结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1515年在她的家乡享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.
German: variant of   Ferch  .

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Roland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, Walloon, German, English, and Scottish: from an ancient Germanic personal name composed of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + land ‘land’. In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the more common French variant Rolland . See also English Rowland .

Norwegian (also Røland): habitational name from any of several farms named Rødland mainly in Hordaland or Røyland mainly in Agder, most of them from Old Norse reyrr ‘heap of stones’ or reyrr ‘rush’ (a plant) + the second element land ‘(piece of) land, farmstead’. Compare Rolland .

Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Roland in Lillehammer, with the first element Old Norse ruth ‘clearing’, or Roland in Agder around Lindesnes, with the first element possibly the Old Norse personal name Rói (either a short form of names beginning with Ro(d)- ‘fame, honor’ or composed with a word for ‘giant’ or ‘pagan priest’). The second element of the farm names is land ‘(piece of) land, farmstead’.

variants.name: Rullan
meanings: Catalan (Rullán): from the personal name Rullán, of ancient Germanic origin (see Roland ).

variants.name: Laurendeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Gaetan, Jacques, Michel, Normand, Philippe, Sylvain.
Altered form of French Rollandeau: variant of Rolandeau, from a pet form of the personal name Roland .
French: from a pet form of (an old vernacular form of) the personal name   Laurent   ‘Lawrence’.

variants.name: Ruhland
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Alfons, Reinhard.
German: from the personal name Ruhland, a common medieval form of Roland .

variants.name: Rohland
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gunter, Gerhard, Otto.
German:
variant of   Roland  .

variants.name: Lora
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Rafael, Ana, Ramon, Mercedes, Altagracia, Fernando, Carlos, Cesar, Ernesto.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Carina, Caesar, Dario, Fausto, Gino, Heriberto, Lorenzo, Marco, Marino, Neri.
Spanish: habitational name from Lora de Estepa or Lora del Río, both in Seville province.
Italian: from the female personal name Lora.

variants.name: Rolland
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Joffre, Francois, Jean-Marc, Lucien, Mathieu, Pascal, Pierre.
French, Breton, Scottish, and English: variant of   Roland  . This is the most common form of the surname in France, especially in Brittany and the south. Compare   Ruland   and   Rulon  .

Norwegian (Rølland): habitational name from either the farm name Rødland, found in two places in Hordaland, most likely a compound of Old Norse reyrr ‘rush’ (a plant) + land ‘(piece of) land, farmstead’, or the farm name Rølland in Agder, from Old Norse ruthland ‘cleared land, clearing’. Compare Rodland and Roland .

variants.name: Roels
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Romain.
Dutch and Flemish: patronymic from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names Roeland (see Roland ) or Roelof (see Roelofs ).

variants.name: Rowland
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Rollant, Rolant, Rolent, Roulent (ancient Germanic Hrodland, Rodland, from hrōd ‘glory’ + land ‘land’), introduced into England by the Normans. It was made famous by French romances about Roland, the most famous of the peers of Charlemagne who was killed at Roncesvalles in AD 778. Although widely used across post-Conquest England (along with Oliver, the name of Roland's companion), it seems to have been restricted to particular gentry families and was never popular. Compare Roland .

English: habitational name from Rowland (Derbyshire) or Rowland Wood in Slinfold (Sussex). The Derbyshire placename derives from Old Norse rá ‘roe, roe buck’ or rá ‘land mark, boundary’ + lúndr ‘small wood, grove’. The Sussex placename probably derives from Middle English roughe ‘rough’ + lond ‘land’ (Old English rūh, land).
English: in northern England and perhaps elsewhere, perhaps a post-medieval variant of   Rawling  . Compare   Rawlinson  ,   Rollinson  .

variants.name: Rolando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Giovanni, Salvo, Valentino, Verino.
Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Rolando, of ancient Germanic origin (see Roland ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: Formed from Margaret.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret verch Roland was born in 1483, in Bodedern, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Rowland ap Howell, was 28 and her mother, Annes ferch Dafydd, was 20. She married Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn in 1502, in Bodedern, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1515, in her hometown, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret verch Roland est née en 1483, à Bodedern, Anglesey, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Rowland ap Howell, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Annes ferch Dafydd, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn en 1502, à Bodedern, Anglesey, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1515, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret verch Roland 1483 in Bodedern, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Rowland ap Howell 28 und ihre Mutter Annes ferch Dafydd 20. Sie hat 1502 in Bodedern, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1515 im Alter von 32 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret verch Roland è nata il nel 1483 nel Bodedern, Anglesey, Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, Rowland ap Howell, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Annes ferch Dafydd, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn nel 1502, nel Bodedern, Anglesey, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1515, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Rolandが1483年にイギリスウェールズアングレセイボデデルンで生まれた時，父Rowland ap Howellは28歳，母Annes ferch Dafyddは20歳であった。1502年にイギリスウェールズアングレセイボデデルンでMeurig Meyrick ap Llywellynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1515年に故郷で32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Roland(이)가 1483년 영국 웨일스 앤글레시 보데더에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rowland ap Howell(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Annes ferch Dafydd(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1502년 영국 웨일스 앤글레시 보데더에서 Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1515년 그녀의 고향에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret verch Roland nasceu em 1483, em Bodedern, Anglesey, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Rowland ap Howell, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Annes ferch Dafydd, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn em 1502, em Bodedern, Anglesey, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1515, em sua cidade natal, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret verch Roland родилась в 1483 г. в местности бодедирн, англеси, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Rowland ap Howell было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Annes ferch Dafydd, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn в 1502 г. в местности бодедирн, англеси, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1515 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 32 лет.

Se casó con Meurig Meyrick ap Llywellyn en 1502, en Bodedern, Isla Oscura, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1515, en su pueblo, a la edad de 32 años.
